T002.change: Added 'files' field listing README.md, BRAND_RULES.md, AGENTS.md, dist, src
references: FR-011: README.md, BRAND_RULES.md, and AGENTS.md MUST be included in the distributed package (spec.md)
SC-006: README.md, BRAND_RULES.md, and AGENTS.md are included in the published package (spec.md)
Quickstart build section: 'cd packages/styles && pnpm build' (quickstart.md)
Quickstart validation: 'pnpm pack --dry-run' should list these files (quickstart.md)

T003.change: Closed unclosed CSS code block with ```css...``` fences; formatted 'Guidance', 'Accessibility', 'License' as ## section headings
references: FR-012: Structural/formatting issues in README.md MUST be corrected (spec.md)
SC-007: README.md renders correctly with no broken code blocks or unformatted section headings (spec.md)
Contract import paths reference: consumers import dist/styles.css via @import or <link> (contracts/README.md)

T004.change: Created .stylelintrc.json with postcss-scss customSyntax and SCSS linting rules (indentation, no-color-literals, no-global-function-names, etc.)
references: SC-001: All tokens compile successfully without errors (spec.md)
Compliance: compiled CSS MUST NOT contain Sass-specific syntax (contracts/README.md)

notes.stylelintrc_design_choice: Used standalone postcss-scss customSyntax instead of 'stylelint-config-standard-scss' extension since that config package is not installed as a dependency. Rules are hand-picked to match stylelint ^16.18.0 built-in rules plus postcss-scss syntax support.
notes.README_fix_details: The Usage section had an implicit CSS example outside any code block; wrapped it in ```css...``` fences. Three headings (Guidance, Accessibility, License) were plain text; prefixed with ## to match document heading hierarchy.
